content_markdown: Which of the following is numerically equal to the specific heat capacity of the substance of a solid body?

options.label: A
options.text: The thermal energy required to melt the body

options.label: B
options.text: The thermal energy required to increase the temperature of unit mass of the body by $1\text{ K}$

options.label: C
options.text: The thermal energy required to increase the temperature of the body by $1\text{ K}$

options.label: D
options.text: The total kinetic and potential energy of all the molecules in the body

vibe_explanation: Specific heat capacity ($c$) is defined by the equation $Q = mc\Delta T$. If we set the mass ($m$) to $1\text{ kg}$ (unit mass) and the change in temperature ($\Delta T$) to $1\text{ K}$, then $Q = c$. Therefore, it is the energy required per unit mass per unit temperature change. Option A refers to latent heat, Option C refers to heat capacity (thermal capacity), and Option D refers to internal energy.
